description: Mexican-American street and prison gang originally based in El Paso, Texas and Ciudad Juarez, Mexico, formed in the early 1980s by Jose 'Raulio' Rivera. It expanded into a transnational criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, murder, and other crimes. The gang has an estimated 8,000 members and is allied with the Juárez Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: The leader of Barrio Azteca, Arturo Gallegos Castrellón, better known for his nickname El Farmero, ordered the execution of two American consulate workers and a Mexican with ties to the agency in Ciudad Juárez on 13 March 2010.
country_links.context: Barrio Azteca operates in Mexico, carrying out killings and drug trafficking, including the U.S. consulate assassinations and the Horizontes del Sur massacre.
